title: Eremogone kansuensis
taxonomy: Eremogone kansuensis is a species of flowering plant in the family Caryophyllaceae, commonly known as the pink or carnation family. This plant is native to Asia and is classified in the kingdom Plantae. It belongs to the genus Eremogone, which includes several species of annual and perennial plants. The genus Eremogone is further classified under the family Caryophyllaceae, and the order Caryophyllales. The classification of Eremogone kansuensis is completed with its full binomial nomenclature as Eremogone kansuensis.

intro: Eremogone kansuensis, also known as Kansu alpine saxifrage, is a perennial plant species belonging to the saxifrage family (Saxifragaceae). This plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, headache, and sore throat, due to its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiviral properties, which contribute to its potential health benefits. The bioactive constituents of Eremogone kansuensis include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Eremogone kansuensis is commonly used to make herbal teas, infusions, and extracts, as well as being incorporated into traditional Chinese medicine formulas.
